title: portulaca pilosa subsp. sundaensis
intro: Portulaca pilosa subsp. sundaensis, also known as purslane, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Portulacaceae family. It is a flowering plant that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, headache, and skin conditions, due to its high concentration of vitamins A and C, and minerals like calcium and iron. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Portulaca pilosa subsp. sundaensis can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its health benefits.
